title: Event
description: An append-only event-ledger entry. Events are the universal join key across runs, tools, artifacts, gates, and releases. Every record is immutable once written; corrections land as a new event with a parent_event_id pointer.

event_id.description: UUID for this event. Unique across the portfolio.

type.description: Dotted namespace event type. Examples: tool.call.completed, agent.run.started, artifact.produced, proof.gate.passed, release.shipped, memory.promoted, decision.recorded, dream.candidate.generated. The canonical vocabulary lives in ops/event-types.md.

created_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the event was recorded.

actor.description: Who or what caused the event.

kind.description: Actor category. role means an agent acting under a role contract; human means a person; system means an automated trigger such as cron.

id.description: Actor identifier. For role, the role id. For human, the handle or email. For system, the trigger name.

payload.description: Event-specific payload. The required fields per type are documented in ops/event-types.md; this schema does not constrain the inner shape so the ledger can absorb new event types without a schema bump.

run_id.description: Run identifier this event belongs to, when applicable.

spec_id.description: Spec identifier this event relates to, when applicable.

artifact_id.description: Artifact identifier this event produced or referenced, when applicable.

cost_usd.description: Dollar cost attributed to this event, when applicable.

tokens_input.description: Input tokens consumed by the action that fired this event, when applicable.

tokens_output.description: Output tokens emitted by the action that fired this event, when applicable.

parent_event_id.description: Event id this one descends from, used to reconstruct causal chains.
